steps.title: Find the y-intercept of each function
steps.content:
To find the y‑coordinate of the y‑intercept, set $$x=0$$ in each definition.
For I: $$f(0)=a(2.1)^0+b=a+b$$.
For II: $$g(0)=a(2.1)^0+b=a+b$$.

steps.title: Check whether the y‑intercept appears as a constant or coefficient
steps.content:
In both expressions the y‑intercept is $$a+b$$, which is a sum of the two parameters. Neither equation shows $$a+b$$ by itself as a standalone constant term or as a coefficient of any term.
Therefore neither I nor II displays the y‑intercept as a constant or coefficient.

steps.title: Select the correct choice
steps.content:
Since neither equation exhibits the y‑intercept directly, the correct option is D) Neither I nor II.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
